title: L11 · Future, Conditional & Subordinate Clauses
objective: Talk about what WILL happen (future), what WOULD happen (conditional), and chain clauses with porque, que, cuando, si. Three tenses + subordinate clauses unlock about 90% of A2 conversation.
strategy: The good news: Spanish future and conditional are FORMED THE SAME WAY (add endings to the full infinitive). The same 12 irregular stems work for BOTH tenses. Drill those once. Then section 4 covers the connector words — they make sentences sound natural.

sections.title: Quick-and-easy future: 'ir + a + infinitive'
sections.note: Before formal future, learn this — it's how Spanish speakers talk about the near future in everyday speech. Same structure as French 'aller + infinitif'.

blocks.text: Conjugate 'ir' in present + 'a' + infinitive of main verb
blocks.audioText: Voy a estudiar. Vas a comer. Va a llover.
blocks.rule: Identical to French 'futur proche' (je vais étudier). Used for plans and predictions in the near future. The main verb stays in infinitive form.

examples.translation: I'm going to study tonight. / Je vais étudier ce soir.
What are you going to do tomorrow? / Qu'est-ce que tu vas faire demain?

examples.text: Vamos a viajar el verano que viene.
examples.audioText: Vamos a viajar el verano que viene.
examples.translation: We're going to travel next summer. / Nous allons voyager l'été prochain.
It's going to rain. / Il va pleuvoir.

sections.title: Formal future (futuro simple)
sections.note: Add endings to the FULL infinitive (don't drop -ar/-er/-ir!). Same endings for all three families. Used for promises, predictions, or hypotheses about the present.

blocks.audioText: hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablaréis, hablarán
blocks.rule: ALL forms have written accents EXCEPT nosotros (-emos). Add directly to the infinitive — no stem change.

examples.translation: I will speak with him tomorrow. / Je lui parlerai demain.
When will they arrive? / Quand arriveront-ils?

blocks.text: 12 IRREGULAR stems (same for future AND conditional)
blocks.audioText: tener → tendré; salir → saldré; venir → vendré; poner → pondré; saber → sabré; haber → habré; poder → podré; querer → querré; hacer → haré; decir → diré
blocks.rule: Memorise these 12 stems — they're identical for future and conditional, just different endings. Three patterns: (1) drop the 'e' (saber→sabré, haber→habré, poder→podré, querer→querré, caber→cabré). (2) replace -e/-i with d (tener→tendré, salir→saldré, venir→vendré, poner→pondré, valer→valdré). (3) special: hacer→haré, decir→diré, satisfacer→satisfaré.

examples.translation: Tomorrow I'll have time. / Demain j'aurai du temps.
I'll do it tonight. / Je le ferai ce soir.

blocks.rule: Future tense in Spanish is ALSO used to express probability/conjecture about the present — 'it's probably X'. Doesn't translate literally to English.

examples.translation: It's probably 5 o'clock. (guessing) / Il doit être 5 heures.
Who could it be? (uncertainty) / Qui ça peut être?

sections.title: Conditional (condicional) — 'would'
sections.note: Used for: politeness, hypotheticals, advice. Same stem as future + imperfect-style endings.

blocks.audioText: hablaría, hablarías, hablaría, hablaríamos, hablaríais, hablarían
blocks.rule: Add to the FULL infinitive (or the irregular stem if there is one). All forms have accents — note the í always has an accent.

examples.translation: Would you speak more slowly, please. / Voudriez-vous parler plus lentement, s'il vous plaît.
I'd like a coffee. / J'aimerais un café. — the polite form of 'me gusta'

examples.text: Tendría más tiempo si trabajara menos.
examples.audioText: Tendría más tiempo si trabajara menos.
examples.translation: I'd have more time if I worked less. / J'aurais plus de temps si je travaillais moins.

blocks.audioText: Me gustaría... ¿Podrías...? Si tuviera dinero, viajaría.
blocks.rule: (1) POLITE requests: ¿Podrías ayudarme? / Could you help me? (2) HYPOTHETICAL: Yo en tu lugar, no iría / If I were you, I wouldn't go. (3) ADVICE: Deberías estudiar más / You should study more.

examples.translation: Could you help me? / Pourrais-tu m'aider? — polite

examples.text: Yo en tu lugar, no iría.
examples.audioText: Yo en tu lugar, no iría.
examples.translation: If I were you, I wouldn't go. / À ta place, je n'irais pas.
You should study more. / Tu devrais étudier plus. — debería = polite 'should'

sections.title: Connectors — chaining ideas
sections.note: These words make sentences flow. Each one has its own quirk.

blocks.audioText: Hablo español y francés. Soy alto pero no muy alto. Café o té. Estudio porque me gusta.
blocks.rule: (1) 'y' becomes 'e' before a word starting with 'i' or 'hi' (Pedro e Iván). (2) 'o' becomes 'u' before 'o' or 'ho' (uno u otro). (3) 'porque' (one word) = because. 'por qué' (two words + accent) = why.

examples.translation: I speak Spanish and French.
Pedro and Iván are friends. — 'y' → 'e' before /i/
I study Spanish because I like it. / J'étudie l'espagnol parce que ça me plaît.
Why are you studying Spanish? — note the accent

blocks.audioText: Creo que tienes razón. Dice que viene mañana.
blocks.rule: Spanish 'que' is NEVER optional (unlike English 'I think (that) you're right'). Always include it.

examples.translation: I think (that) you're right. / Je crois que tu as raison.
He says he's coming tomorrow. / Il dit qu'il vient demain.
It's important that you study. / Il est important que tu étudies. — note: triggers subjunctive (next lesson!)

blocks.text: cuando = 'when' — same word for past and future
blocks.audioText: Cuando era pequeño, vivía en Madrid. Te llamo cuando llegue.
blocks.rule: Past time: 'cuando' + indicative (cuando era pequeño). FUTURE time: 'cuando' + SUBJUNCTIVE (cuando llegue, not cuando llego). Critical rule.

examples.text: Cuando era niño, vivía en Madrid.
examples.audioText: Cuando era niño, vivía en Madrid.
examples.translation: When I was a child, I lived in Madrid. (past — indicative)
I'll call you when I arrive. / Je t'appellerai quand j'arriverai. — Spanish requires SUBJUNCTIVE ('llegue', not 'llego') here. Reason: future uncertainty. You'll master this in L12.

blocks.audioText: Si tengo tiempo, voy. Si tuviera tiempo, iría.
blocks.rule: Three types: (1) REAL: 'si + present, + present/future' = if X happens, Y happens. (2) HYPOTHETICAL (present): 'si + imperfect subj, + conditional' = if X happened, Y would happen. (3) PAST: 'si + pluperfect subj, + cond. perfect'. You'll see (1) and (3) here; (2) needs subjunctive (L12).

examples.text: Si tengo tiempo, voy al cine.
examples.audioText: Si tengo tiempo, voy al cine.
examples.translation: If I have time, I'll go to the cinema. (real / likely) / Si j'ai le temps, je vais au cinéma.
If it rains, we don't go out. / S'il pleut, nous ne sortons pas.

blocks.rule: 'mientras' + ongoing action. Often paired with imperfect.

examples.translation: While I was talking on the phone, I was eating. / Pendant que je parlais au téléphone, je mangeais.

blocks.rule: Concession — contrast with the main clause. Like English 'even though'.

examples.translation: Even though it's raining, we're going out. / Bien qu'il pleuve, nous sortons.
